content.full_text:
FPPC

Fair Political Practices
Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

MAY 07 1992

May 6,1992

The purpose of this letter is to seek clarification on what
consulting activities I may engage in as a former state official.
In February, I vacated my position as Chief Deputy Director for
Operations within the California Department of Parks and Recreation.

Recently, I received a letter from the Department stating that
they require an advice letter from your office for me to
deal with any of their employees (see attached). I have reviewed
with my attorney the code sections regarding activities of former
officials as well as an advice letter which you issued to another
former official. I am clear that former officials are not to lobby
their former agency or consult on matters which they participated
in while in state government. My interest is not in lobbying or
getting involved in projects or matters which I previously parti-
cipated in, but rather to provide consulting services to people
who can benefit from my knowledge gained over the years in state
and local government. It would seem that I should have a right
to use my expertise to advise clients, conduct research on their
behalf, and arrange meetings for them with state officials, inclu-
ding those at State Parks.

I request that you provide me with a general advice letter on
what is and is not allowed for former officials. Also, an advice
letter is requested for each of the matters described below (per
the DPR requirement)

An association of individuals who do business with State Parks has
ask me to manage the affairs of their association. They have also
ask that I monitor the activities of the Department to the extent
that I can advise them if there are pending plans that should concern
them. They realize that I cannot represent them for the purpose
of trying to influence but rather to review public information and
advise the Association.

A private concessionaire wishes to establish new concessions
within the state park system and has asked for my opinion on what
might represent the best opportunities. While he may ask me to
help draft proposals, he understands that I cannot represent any
proposals before the Department. Any contact with the Department
would be for the purpose of collecting public information and
nothing more.

Cont.


To provide an accurate transcription following your rules, I would need a clearer, higher contrast version of this document.

An existing state park concessionaire has asked that I monitor
a DPR planning program underway at the park where his business
is located. He has asked that I advise him on any planned
changes by the Department which could have an effect on his
business. While at DPR I had no involvement with this planning
project, nor do I plan to try and influence its outcome.

A local government agency is in the process of accepting a
State Recreation Area for local operation. They have asked that
I assist them in preparing a Lake Management Plan in anticipation
of taking over operations. I do not plan to represent the agency
on any other matters related to the proposed transfer. My only
contact with the DPR would be for the purpose of collecting public
information useable to the agency. The work does not involve
trying to influence the state but rather to share my knowledge
on lake operations.

Finally, I am interested in knowing if I could advise on and/or
help companies or individuals draft proposals/bids in response
to request for proposals which are sent out by the DPR, if it
involved a project which I did not previously participate in as
a state official. I anticipate my only contact with the DPR
would be for collecting public information and not for trying to
influence in any way.

Your prompt reply to this letter will be appreciated.

Sincerely,

Jack Harrison
Jack Harrison
4213 Galewood Way
Carmichael, CA 95608
(916) 944-1833

cc: Donald W. Murphy
    Director
    Department of Parks
    and Recreation

STATE OF CALIFORNIA — RESOURCES AGENCY                                                    PETE WILSON, Governor

DEPARTMENT OF PARKS AND RECREATION
P.O. BOX 942896
SACRAMENTO 94296-0001

(916) 653-8380


MAR - 4 1992


Mr. Jack V. Harrison
Harrison and Associates
1127 - Eleventh Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Dear Jack:

     The issue has been raised as to the propriety of this Department dealing
with former executives as representatives of private entities who seek to do
business with this Department. I have looked into the matter and found that
the issue revolves around an interpretation of Government Code Section 87406,
the Milton Marks Post Government Employment Restrictions Act of 1990,
Government Code Section 87406(d)(1). Under Government Code Section 91003, a
decision made by this Department or a contract issued could be voided by
injunction, and the complainant could receive attorney's fees which can be
substantial (Downey Cares vs. Downey Community Development Com. (1987) 196
Cal. App. 3d 983).

     Since all of these matters are in the area of jurisdiction by the Fair
Political Practices Commission, and the government code provides for the staff
of the Commission to issue advice letters, I am requesting that you secure
advice letters concerning the matters in which you deal with the Department,
under Government Code Section 83114(b).

     Since the exposure is significant, I am setting a Department policy that
our employees not deal with persons falling within the prohibitions of
Government Code Section 87406 unless such an advice letter is available. I
realize this will have substantial impact given the current reorganization of
the Department, but the alternatives are less palatable in today's climate for
litigation.

                                                    Sincerely,

                                                    [signature]

                                                    Donald W. Murphy
                                                    Director

L-0948x

[California State Parks logo at bottom]
